headline: Owens Corning — $250M, 30-year HQ stay + Riverwalk easement: council vote approved
paragraphs: Toledo City Council approved the measure to keep Owens Corning in Toledo, formalizing a deal first announced in early April. In exchange for the property-tax incentive extension, Owens Corning will invest $250M in building and technology improvements, create 25 new jobs, maintain 1,036 existing high-wage jobs, and commit to keeping its world HQ downtown for 30 more years.

The approved deal bundles three separate agreements: a 30-year TIF extension with the Lucas County Port Authority, a payment-in-lieu-of-taxes agreement with Toledo Public Schools, and a separate agreement with the city and Metroparks Toledo granting the final phase of the Glass City Riverwalk access across the Owens Corning campus. That third piece — the Riverwalk easement — is now locked in law rather than pending negotiation.
why_it_matters: The campus easement is the under-reported detail in this deal — it means the Riverwalk's terminal phase is no longer contingent on a future negotiation with a corporate landowner. With council approval confirmed, that tail risk is removed. The $250M investment commitment and the 1,036 retained jobs are the headline numbers, but the Riverwalk easement is what makes the full 2032 buildout structurally bankable. Both the stadium group and the Vistula Metropark team now have a cleaner path to market against a completed Riverwalk corridor.
search_terms: Owens Corning Toledo headquarters tax incentive 250 million 30 year Riverwalk easement TPS PILOT council vote approved
